actions.name: Add key
actions.description: Add a new key to storage, with a given value. If the key already exists, its value is updated.

actions.name: Clear
actions.description: Remove all keys from storage, making the object empty.

actions.name: Delete key
actions.description: Remove a key and its value from storage. If the key does not exist, this has no effect.

actions.name: Set key
actions.description: Update the value for a key which already exists. If the key does not exist, this has no effect. (Unlike Add key, the key will not be created.)

actions.name: Download
actions.description: Invokes a browser download of a file containing the Dictionary's contents in JSON format.

actions.name: Load
actions.description: Load all keys and values from JSON data previously retrieved from the Dictionary object using either the Download action, the AsJSON expression, or the AJAX object loading a project file. Note this does not allow loading arbitrary JSON data - the data must be in a special format for Construct. If you want to load data from a project file, create it using the New - Dictionary menu option in the Project Bar.

conditions.name: Compare value
conditions.description: Compare the value stored for a key.

conditions.name: Has key
conditions.description: Check if a key exists in storage.

conditions.name: Is empty
conditions.description: True when there are no keys in storage.

conditions.name: For each key
conditions.description: Repeat the event once for each key in storage. The CurrentKey and CurrentValue expressions return the current key and its value respectively.

conditions.name: Compare current value
conditions.description: Only valid in a For each key event. Compare the value of the current key.

expressions.name: Get(key)
expressions.description: Return the value stored for a key, e.g. Dictionary.Get("score"). If the key does not exist, it returns 0.

expressions.name: GetDefault(key, valueIfMissing)
expressions.description: Return the value stored for a key, but if it is missing, return a different value instead. For example Dictionary.GetDefault("name", "guest") will return the value of the key "name" if it exists, otherwise it will return the string "guest".

expressions.name: KeyCount
expressions.description: Return the number of keys in storage.

expressions.name: CurrentKey
expressions.description: In a For each key event, these return the key and its value (respectively) for the current key being iterated.

expressions.name: CurrentValue
expressions.description: Return the contents of the Dictionary object in JSON format. This can be later loaded back with the Load action, sent to a server via AJAX, saved to disk, and so on.
